purpose: Defines the `!reset [dashboardId | --all]` command for clearing dashboards. Single-dashboard mode: build a history summary (with lessons_learned: deviations, failure_causes, warnings, patterns), write to history/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}.json, MANDATORILY archive the entire dashboard directory to Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/, then clear progress/*.json files and reset initialization.json+logs.json to empty state. --all mode: iterate every dashboard from listDashboards() applying the same flow.
gotchas: Archive is MANDATORY — never skip; copies entire dashboard directory before clearing
Already-empty dashboards (task: null) must report 'already empty' and stop — do not archive empty state
History summary includes a `lessons_learned` object with 4 nested fields (deviations[], failure_causes[], warnings[], patterns) — different from the regular history summary used by the server
History filename format: {YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}.json — must match the convention used by HistoryService.js
Empty initialization.json after reset has shape: { task: null, agents: [], waves: [], chains: [], history: [] } — note the empty arrays
Empty logs.json after reset has shape: { entries: [] }
TASK_NAME shell extraction uses grep+head+cut — fragile to JSON formatting changes; archive name fallback is ${TASK_NAME:-unnamed}
patterns: 6-step single-dashboard procedure: parse → check empty → build+save history summary → archive → clear progress + reset init+logs → report
--all mode replays steps 3-5 across all dashboards skipping empty ones
Two artifacts per reset: history JSON + Archive directory copy (both required)
conventions: Two parallel sub-sections: Single Dashboard Reset and Reset All (--all)
Bash code block for archive copy operation with TASK_NAME extraction
History summary fields enumerated as bulleted list with nested fields
